howItWorks.intro: This absolute value equation solver uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Case 1: ax + b = c → x = (c - b)/a
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: Why are there two solutions to an absolute value equation?
faq.answer: Absolute value measures distance from zero, so |expression| = c means the expression equals either c or -c. This creates two separate linear equations to solve.

faq.question: When does an absolute value equation have no solution?
faq.answer: When c is negative. Since absolute value is always non-negative (≥ 0), |anything| = negative number is impossible.

faq.question: How do I check my answers?
faq.answer: Substitute each solution back into the original equation and verify that both sides are equal. Sometimes one solution may be extraneous if the original problem had restrictions.
